entries.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
entries.snippet: Tehran has not commented on the incident, which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

entries.title: Eight children killed in Louisiana shooting, police say
entries.snippet: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

entries.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
entries.snippet: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

entries.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
entries.snippet: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

entries.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
entries.snippet: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

entries.title: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
entries.snippet: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

entries.title: Rumen Radev looks set to win Bulgarian Parliamentary election
entries.snippet: Exit polls put his Progressive Bulgaria on 37%, more than double the score of the closest runner-up, but he will likely need to form a coalition to govern.

entries.title: Thousands of Parisians evacuated as WW2 bomb detonated
entries.snippet: Residents within a 450m radius of the device were told to leave their homes while it was neutralised.

entries.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford agrees to sell $21m private jet following outcry
entries.snippet: After confirming the purchase on Friday, Ford drew critcism from political opponents who called it a "gravy plane" and "out of touch".

entries.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
entries.snippet: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

entries.title: Alec Baldwin to face civil trial over Rust film set shooting
entries.snippet: The criminal case against the actor was dismissed in 2024.

entries.title: Bodies of 50 infants dumped at Trinidad graveyard
entries.snippet: Police say a preliminary investigation shows it may be a case of an "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses".

entries.title: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
entries.snippet: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

entries.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum denies 'diplomatic crisis' with Spain after conquest row
entries.snippet: The Mexican president says there were never tensions over Spanish colonisation, which had become a thorny issue.

entries.title: Building of Trump's White House ballroom can resume in full, appeals court says
entries.snippet: Construction at the White House is expected to continue until the next hearing, due in June.

entries.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
entries.snippet: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

entries.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
entries.snippet: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

entries.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
entries.snippet: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

entries.title: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
entries.snippet: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

entries.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
entries.snippet: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

entries.title: Communities launch cleanup after severe weather and tornadoes churn across Midwest
entries.snippet: At least 66 tornado reports were submitted across multiple states on Friday, according to the National Weather Service.

entries.title: Will the DHS shutdown affect security for the World Cup?
entries.snippet: The FIFA World Cup is a little over 50 days away. NPR's Rob Schmitz talks to former Department of Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about the security concerns people have about hosting the tournament in America in this moment.

entries.title: 8 children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
entries.snippet: The suspect was the father of seven of the children killed, police said. The victims ranged in age from 1 to about 14 years old, according to police in Shreveport, La. A total of 10 people were shot.

entries.title: Why nearly every farmer who grows these chile peppers is a woman
entries.snippet: Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."

entries.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

entries.title: Real estate investors are buying up long-term care facilities. Residents can suffer
entries.snippet: Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.

entries.title: This tariff-refund portal is about to be America's hottest website
entries.snippet: Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.

entries.title: She no longer remembers it's her birthday. He got her a present anyway
entries.snippet: A special day can be tinged with sorrow when your partner has dementia. But then he found the perfect gift.

entries.title: Iowa went all-in on school choice. It's hurting this city's public schools
entries.snippet: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.

entries.title: The Little Probe That Could: Why Voyager 1 Matters, and Why NASA Just Switched Part of It Off
entries.snippet: This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.

entries.title: Oil prices spike again following latest standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: The ongoing conflict with the U.S. and Iran prevented tankers from passing through. The price of U.S. crude oil increased 6.4% to $87.88 per barrel after trading resumed on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. The price of Brent crude, the international standard, climbed 6.5% to $96.

entries.title: Los Angeles woman arrested on suspicion of helping Iran traffic weapons to Sudan
entries.snippet: First U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli said Sunday on social media that the woman will face charges that she brokered the sale of "drones, bombs, bomb fuses, and millions of rounds of ammunition" between Iran and the Sudanese Armed Forces.

entries.title: Canada's prime minister says economic connection to the U.S. has shifted from a strength to a weakness
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Mark Carney said tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump have impacted workers in such major industries as auto and steel and prompted businesses to hold back investments.

entries.title: Chernobyl full of life as wildlife reoccupies a radioactive landscape
entries.snippet: Wolves, bears and lynx have rebounded in the radioactive landscape, along with a rare breed of horses native to Mongolia. Scientists say it shows nature's ability to recover when human activity is removed.

entries.title: U.S. invests in project to remove rare earth minerals despite differences with South Africa
entries.snippet: The U.S. has committed a $50 million investment in the Phalaborwa Rare Earths Project despite U.S.-South Africa relations plummeting to their lowest point in decades.

entries.title: Police investigating arson attacks at Jewish sites in London
entries.snippet: British police say they are looking at whether the incidents are the work of Iranian proxies.

entries.title: Former president Rumen Radev will win Bulgaria election, exit poll suggests
entries.snippet: SOFIA, Bulgaria (AP) — An exit poll in Bulgaria suggested Sunday that the center-left Progressive Bulgaria coalition led by ex-president Rumen Radev will be the winner of the country's parliamentary election. The poll conducted by Trend research group showed Radev's coalition ear

entries.title: 5 people shot during fight in nightlife district near the University of Iowa, police say
entries.snippet: Authorities in Iowa say three University of Iowa students have been wounded in a shooting that erupted in a nightlife district near campus. Two others were injured in the shooting that happened early Sunday in downtown Iowa City.

entries.title: Russian strikes kill at least 2 as Ukraine targets drone factory inside Russia
entries.snippet: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian strikes killed at least two people in Ukraine, officials said Sunday, as the Ukrainian military struck a drone factory in southwestern Russia. A "massive" nighttime drone strike on Chernihiv in northern Ukraine killed a 16-year-old boy and wounded fou

entries.title: Gunman kills 8 children in Louisiana mass shooting
entries.snippet: Officials say a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children, including seven of his own, and shot two other people Sunday. Police say the victims ranged in age from 1 to about 12 years old and one of the adults wounded was the mother of the suspect's children.

entries.title: Humanoid robot wins Beijing half-marathon, defeating the human world record
entries.snippet: This year's race featured over 300 robots from more than 100 teams, including international participants. The competition highlights China's advancements in a technological competition with the U.S.

entries.title: Eddie Murphy receives AFI life achievement award along with star-studded tribute
entries.snippet: The American Film Institute ceremony, held Saturday night at the Dolby Theatre, featured appearances from Spike Lee, Martin Lawrence, Dave Chappelle, and many more.

entries.title: FBI and Justice Department try to rebuild after wave of resignations and firings
entries.snippet: The agencies leaders are easing hiring requirements and accelerating recruitment in ways that some current and former officials see as a lowering of long-accepted standards.

entries.title: North Korea tests short-range ballistic missiles
entries.snippet: South Korea's military was analyzing whether the latest launches were made from a submarine, a land-based launcher or both platforms, according to South Korean media.

entries.title: U.S. attacks Iranian-flagged ship as Tehran promises to respond
entries.snippet: It was the first interception since the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports began last week. Iran's joint military command called the armed boarding an act of piracy and a ceasefire violation, the state broadcaster said.

entries.title: How consumers can navigate the cost of available flights impacted by the Iran war
entries.snippet: Experts say booking earlier, and staying flexible on dates, destinations and departure airports can help reduce costs.

entries.title: Obama and Mamdani meet for the first time
entries.snippet: The meeting comes as the New York mayor, a democratic socialist, is also trying to build a working relationship with Republican President Donald Trump. The meeting comes just over a week after Mamdani marked his 100th day in office.

entries.title: Witnesses detail deadly Israeli strikes on medics in Lebanon
entries.snippet: The three strikes, which killed four paramedics and wounded six others, is the latest example of Israel's willingness to target Lebanon's health sector. The attacks on the ambulances have drawn condemnation, including from the United Nations' human rights office.

entries.title: Arizona midterms begin with spotlight on largest county's controversial election system
entries.snippet: Much of the political spotlight this election year in Arizona has been focused on the office that runs elections in Maricopa County, the state's largest. Republican Justin Heap is overseeing elections in the Phoenix-area and he's an election skeptic.

entries.title: How to spot the Lyrid meteor shower that is peaking this week
entries.snippet: The show will be visible across the globe, but views will be best in the Northern Hemisphere. And there's no risk of the crescent moon photobombing the Lyrid shower. It'll set before the fun starts.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Bayern Munich clinch 35th Bundesliga title
entries.snippet: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

entries.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russian Radev leading after parliamentary vote, exit polls show
entries.snippet: Exit polls put Rumen Radev's new, left-leaning, pro-Russian party far ahead following Bulgaria's eighth election in five years. Radev, a former fighter pilot, stepped down as Bulgaria's president in January.

entries.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
entries.snippet: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

entries.title: US mass shooting leaves 8 children dead in Louisiana
entries.snippet: A "domestic disturbance" in the US state of Louisiana has claimed the lives of eight children, including a 1-year-old, police have said.

entries.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
entries.snippet: Iran says it has placed mines in the Straight of Hormuz to discourage independent traffic. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

entries.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
entries.snippet: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

entries.title: India's harvest festivals under climate strain
entries.snippet: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

entries.title: Malaysia: Fire in impoverished village destroys 1,000 homes
entries.snippet: The fire ripped through makeshift homes in a "water village" in Malaysia's Sabah ‌state ⁠on ⁠Borneo island. The area is home to some of the country's poorest residents, including indigenous and stateless communities.

entries.title: French billionaire defiant as authors quit top publisher
entries.snippet: Vincent Bollore has vowed to find new authors for publisher Grasset after 170 left, alleging political interference. French President Macron has weighed in on the affair, which has shaken France's cultural scene.

entries.title: German crime statistics: false blame on migrants?
entries.snippet: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

entries.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
entries.snippet: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

entries.title: Robot runners beat humans in Beijing half-marathon
entries.snippet: For the first time, humanoid robots have outclassed humans in a half-marathon held in Beijing. The winner beat the men's record for the distance set by Uganda’s Jacob Kiplimo in March.

entries.title: Attacks on US academics: A microcosm of a larger threat to democracy
entries.snippet: In the new DW documentary "Democracy Under Attack: Can Dündar and Trump's America," the Turkish press freedom icon looks at the parallels between the erosion of democracy in the US and his home country.

entries.title: India protests Iran fire on two vessels in Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: New Delhi summoned Iran's envoy after two India-flagged vessels were attacked while attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: Bodies of 50 babies, 6 adults found in Trinidad and Tobago
entries.snippet: Police made the grim discovery at a cemetery in Cumuto on the island of Trinidad. Initial probes suggest it was a case of "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses."

entries.title: Spain, Brazil, Mexico vow to boost Cuba aid amid US threats
entries.snippet: The pledge comes amid a US oil embargo and Donald Trump's threats to invade the island. Leftist leaders meeting in Barcelona also spoke against the global far-right threat.

entries.title: Ukraine: Gunman shot dead after killing spree in Kyiv
entries.snippet: The mass shooting, in a busy central district of the Ukrainian capital, left 14 others wounded, officials said.

entries.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
entries.snippet: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

entries.title: Pope Leo plays down rift with Trump, doubles down on peace
entries.snippet: En route to Angola for a three-day visit, Pope Leo XIV said he has no desire to debate with US President Donald Trump. The two leaders have traded barbs since the pope spoke out against the Iran war.
